chunks.text: Corporate Access Program (CAP) Our Corporate Access Program (CAP) provides exclusive access to EECS students through on-campus recruiting opportunities, events, and personalized staff support. A partnership with Berkeley EECS through CAP is the most seamless way to recruit on campus. The Corporate Access Program was established to aid companies in recruiting and forming connections with students. CAP assists companies in: Managing competitive on-campus recruiting of EECS/CS students. Securing departmental space and staff time, alongside offering soft funding to student organizations and other departmental priorities.
chunks.question: What is the purpose of the Corporate Access Program?
chunks.answer: To provide exclusive recruiting access to EECS students.

chunks.text: CAP assists students in fostering on-campus interactions with potential employers, ensuring these interactions are effectively organized, appropriately timed, and aligned with the university’s public mission. CAP is most suitable for companies capable of frequent campus visits, adept at forming relationships with students in informal environments, and equipped with a strong system for gathering data and managing applicants. Companies that can highlight and leverage Berkeley alumni as recruiting partners are ideal matches.
chunks.question: What type of companies are ideal for CAP?
chunks.answer: Those with frequent campus visits and strong applicant systems.

chunks.text: CAP Membership Benefits Develop your on-campus recruitment strategy tailored to your recruiting needs, including: A dedicated staff relationship manager to help you utilize the program benefits, customize your recruitment strategy, and serve as your entry point into UC Berkeley via EECS. At least two recruitment-focused events with a student organization’s consultation, partnership, and logistical assistance. Access to invite-only career fairs: The EECS Career Fair and EECS Internship Fair, in early September. On-campus interviews, office hours, and access to department resources.
chunks.question: What are key CAP membership benefits?
chunks.answer: Dedicated manager, events, and invite-only career fairs.

chunks.text: CAP At-A-Glance ~3,000 students attended CAP events in 2023-2024 22 CAP events in 2023-2024 On-Campus Recruiting Info sessions, tech talks, tabling, and interviews. CAP Annual Membership A $20,000 gift to the EECS Industrial Liaison Fund, annually, which funds student organizations, and special initiatives. 2025-2026 Student Fairs EECS Career Fair (Careers/Full-Time roles ONLY) – September 4, 2024 EECS Internship Fair (Internship roles ONLY) – September 5, 2024
chunks.question: What is the annual membership cost for CAP?
chunks.answer: $20,000 gift to the Industrial Liaison Fund.

chunks.text: For more information, please contact: James Greene External Relations Coordinator External Relations Group (XRG) 231 Cory Hall #1770 University of California Berkeley, CA 94720 Email: cap-admin@eecs.berkeley.edu
chunks.question: Who is the contact for CAP information?
